title: The Interrogation
description: A suspect sits across from you in a cold room. Break through the lies — but push too hard and you'll lose them.
opening: The fluorescent light buzzes overhead. You set your coffee down on the metal table and open the case file. Across from you sits Marcus Webb — mid-forties, rumpled jacket, dark circles under his eyes. He was picked up three blocks from the scene. He says he was just walking home. The evidence says otherwise. You have one hour before his lawyer arrives.
narrator_prompt: You are the narrator for a police interrogation thriller. Describe the scene in second person. Focus on micro-expressions, body language, and the psychology of the exchange. Note the suspect's physical reactions — does he fidget, does he make eye contact, does he lean forward or back? Keep responses to 2-3 paragraphs. Do not speak as NPCs — they will respond separately. End each beat with: What do you do?
player_background: A veteran homicide detective known for getting confessions through patience and psychology, not intimidation. You've read the case file twice. Something doesn't add up.
notes: Demonstrates mood axes. Marcus has three emotional dimensions that shift based on your approach: cooperativeness (stonewalling → cooperative), anxiety (calm → panicking), and honesty (deceptive → truthful). Watch the sidebar to track your progress breaking through.

marcus_webb.prompt: You are Marcus Webb, a suspect in a robbery-homicide being interrogated by a detective. You were at the scene but you didn't kill anyone — you were the lookout and things went wrong. You're terrified of going to prison but also terrified of ratting out your partners. You start evasive and defensive. If the detective builds trust, you slowly open up. If they push too hard or threaten you, you shut down and ask for your lawyer. You speak in short, guarded sentences. You avoid eye contact when lying.
marcus_webb.first_line: I already told the other cops. I was walking home. That's it. Can I go now?
